manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: decongestant, insurgence, shuffler, simplification, pegboard, seamlessness, pleader, electrostatics, signifier.
Your opponent's team words are: mudslinger, dangerousness, multiprocessing, emancipationist, catheterization, cashbook, linesman, cataloger.
Distractor words are: decentralization, plurality, unconstitutionality, moviegoer, categorization, lightheadedness, tragicomedy.
The kill words are: backwardness.

message.reasoning:
We need to output a single-word clue and target list. We must choose clue relating to some of our team's words. Must avoid being any word from any list (including opponent, distractors, kill). Also clue cannot contain parts of them. Need to be as short as possible.

Team words: decongestant, insurgence, shuffler, simplification, peg...
Let's think common theme: maybe "streamline" for simplification, seamlessness, decongestant. Could be "smooth". "Smooth" relates to seamlessness, simplification, shuffler? shuffler is movement. Maybe "ease". "Ease" relates to simplification, decongestant (eases congestion), seamlessness (ease). Also "pleader" (ease? not). "electrostatics" maybe "charge". "signifier" maybe "meaning". Possibly clue "clarity"? hmm.

Need to choose clue that doesn't accidentally match opponent words (e.g., "dangerousness" etc). Avoid kill "backwardness". Also clue cannot be part of any words. So "smooth" is not present. Does smooth relate to our words? seamlessness obviously, simplification (smooth process), decongestant (smooth breathing?). Might be okay.

But better maybe "flow". Flow relates to decongestant (helps flow), insurgence? maybe not. Shuffler flow. Simplification flow. Seamlessness flow. Signifier flow? maybe.

We can select multiple target words but must be safe. Choose 2 or 3.

Clue "smooth" -> target: seamlessness, simplification, decongestant? Possibly.

Will the partner guess correctly? Should avoid opponent words like "dangerousness". smooth not present. Good.

Thus answer:

CLUE: smooth
TARGETS: seamlessness, simplification, decongestant

Make sure formatting exact
